headline: Beluga Whales Freed After Two Years at Closed Theme Park
meta_description: Thirty beluga whales have been successfully rescued from a closed theme park where they were trapped for two years, marking a major animal welfare victory.
summary_short: After two years confined at a shuttered facility, 30 beluga whales have finally been rescued to safety, bringing relief to animal advocates worldwide.
body:
Thirty beluga whales have been rescued from a closed theme park where they had been stuck for two years. The rescue represents a significant achievement for animal welfare organizations and conservationists who worked to secure the freedom of these marine mammals.

Beluga whales are highly intelligent and social creatures that thrive in open ocean environments. Their prolonged confinement in an inactive facility highlighted ongoing concerns about the use of marine mammals in entertainment venues. The successful rescue operation demonstrates growing momentum in efforts to transition such animals to sanctuaries where they can live more naturally.

The release of these whales marks an important step toward ending the captivity of marine mammals for commercial purposes, offering hope that similar animals in difficult circumstances may also find their way to freedom.
